info.title: Integration API - Server to Extole
info.description: Server-to-Extole integration endpoints: event submission, person lookup, zone rendering, token management, and reward retrieval for backend services authenticating with client credentials.

get.description: Returns reward records for the client matching the specified query parameters.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filter by reward state(s). Repeat for multiple values (e.g. `EARNED`, `FULFILLED`).

parameters.name: reward_supplier_id
parameters.description: Filter by Extole reward supplier id(s). Repeat for multiple values.

parameters.name: partner_reward_supplier_id
parameters.description: Filter by partner-assigned reward supplier id(s). Repeat for multiple values.

parameters.name: person_id
parameters.description: Filter by Extole person id(s). Repeat for multiple values.

parameters.name: action_id
parameters.description: Filter by Extole action id(s). Repeat for multiple values.

parameters.name: root_event_id
parameters.description: Filter by root event id(s). Repeat for multiple values.

parameters.name: partner_reward_id
parameters.description: Filter by partner-assigned reward id(s). Repeat for multiple values.

parameters.name: reward_type
parameters.description: Filter by reward type(s). Repeat for multiple values.

parameters.name: success_only
parameters.description: When true (default), include only rewards that were successfully fulfilled.

parameters.name: time_interval
parameters.description: ISO 8601 time interval to restrict results to a date range (e.g. `P30D` for last 30 days).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of rewards to return. Defaults to 100.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of rewards to skip for pagination. Defaults to 0.

post.description: Marks the custom reward as failed, recording a fulfilment failure.
Marks the custom reward as fulfilled, signalling that the client system has completed fulfilment.

post.summary: Mark custom reward fulfilled and sent
post.description: Marks the custom reward as fulfilled and sent to the participant.
Marks the custom reward as redeemed by the participant.
Marks the custom reward as sent to the participant.

get.description: Returns aggregate state counts for rewards on this client (e.g. earned, fulfilled, cancelled).

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Time period for grouping (e.g. `DAY`, `WEEK`, `MONTH`).

parameters.name: period_count
parameters.description: Number of periods to include in the summary.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End of the date range for the summary.

parameters.name: success_only
parameters.description: When true, include only rewards that were successfully fulfilled.

parameters.name: reward_supplier_id
parameters.description: Filter by one or more reward supplier ids (repeat the parameter for multiple values).

parameters.name: reward_type
parameters.description: Filter by one or more reward types (repeat the parameter for multiple values).

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X GET "https://api.extole.io/v2/rewards/state_summary" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)
Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
response = requests.get(
    "https://api.extole.io/v2/rewards/state_summary",
    headers={"Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN", "Accept": "application/json"},
)
response.raise_for_status()
print(response.json())

get.description: Returns the metadata for the access token used to authenticate the request (`type`, `client_id`, `identity_id`, `expires_in`, `scopes`). Equivalent to `GET /v4/tokens/{token}` but without having to repeat the token in the URL path.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X GET "https://api.extole.io/v4/tokens" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)
Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

response = requests.get(
    "https://api.extole.io/v4/tokens",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN",
        "Accept": "application/json",
    },
)
response.raise_for_status()
token = response.json()
print(token["client_id"], token["expires_in"], token["scopes"])

post.description: Mints a bearer token for server-to-Extole calls by a client. The body is optional: when omitted, the new token mirrors the calling identity's scopes; when supplied, the body can narrow the scope set (subset of the caller's scopes), bind the token to a specific `client_id`, supply email/password credentials in lieu of a calling token, or override the default lifetime via `duration_seconds`. Returns the new token, its `expires_in` (seconds), the resolved `client_id`, the `identity_id` of the user the token represents, and the granted `scopes`.

400.description: Bad request. The named examples below cover this operation's input-validation errors. Other 400 causes include malformed JSON and missing required fields - inspect the response `code` field for the specific error.

403.description: Authentication failed. The named examples below cover the most common credential rejections; other 403 causes (account locked, account disabled, requested scopes that exceed the calling identity's privileges) are auto-derived - inspect the response `code` field for the specific error.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.extole.io/v4/tokens" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "client_id": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
    "email": "integration-bot@example.com",
    "password": "PASSWORD",
    "duration_seconds": 86400
  }'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)
Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

response = requests.post(
    "https://api.extole.io/v4/tokens",
    headers={
        "Content-Type": "application/json",
        "Accept": "application/json",
    },
    json={
        "client_id": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID",
        "email": "integration-bot@example.com",
        "password": "PASSWORD",
        "duration_seconds": 86400,
    },
)
response.raise_for_status()
token = response.json()
print(token["access_token"], token["expires_in"], token["scopes"])

put.description: Exchanges the supplied access token for a fresh one with the same scopes and a renewed expiry. The original token is invalidated immediately on success. Use this to rotate long-lived integration tokens without re-authenticating.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X PUT "https://api.extole.io/v4/tokens/exchange/TOKEN_VALUE" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)
Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

response = requests.put(
    "https://api.extole.io/v4/tokens/exchange/TOKEN_VALUE",
    headers={"Accept": "application/json"},
)
response.raise_for_status()
refreshed = response.json()
print(refreshed["access_token"], refreshed["expires_in"])

get.description: Searches for persons by identity key value or client-defined person keys (supply in `name:value` format, e.g. `partner_conversion_id:abc123`). Returns an array because the same key may resolve to multiple Extole profiles. Supply at least one search criterion via query parameters.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X GET "https://api.extole.io/v5/persons?email=user%40example.com" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)
Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests
response = requests.get(
    "https://api.extole.io/v5/persons",
    params={"email": "user@example.com"},
    headers={"Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN", "Accept": "application/json"},
)
response.raise_for_status()
print(len(response.json()))

post.description: Creates a new person profile in the Extole platform and returns the persisted record with its server-assigned id. Use this to provision person profiles outside of the normal event-tracking flow (e.g. bulk imports).

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST "https://api.extole.io/v5/persons" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "newuser@example.com", "first_name": "Jane", "last_name": "Doe"}'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript (fetch)
Python (requests)
x-codeSamples.source:

response = requests.post(
    "https://api.extole.io/v5/persons",
    headers={
        "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN",

        "Accept": "application/json",
    },
    json={"email": "newuser@example.com", "first_name": "Jane", "last_name": "Doe"},

get.description: Returns the set of partner key names configured for the calling client. Partner keys are client-defined person identifier types (e.g. `partner_conversion_id`, `partner_shipment_id`) that supplement Extole's built-in identifiers. Supply the optional `include_campaign_ignoring_state` parameter to include partner keys declared in a specific campaign regardless of that campaign's state.

post.description: Renders a zone using a request body that carries both the zone name and the targeting data. Functionally equivalent to `POST /v5/zones/{zone_name}`; the path-based form is the recommended primary entry point. Use this form when the calling code constructs the full request payload upstream and prefers a single body shape. Returns the zone's configured content (HTML, JavaScript, JSON, or plain text) plus `X-Extole-*` headers describing the matched campaign and creative. Authenticated with a USER_SUPPORT scope access token.

200.description: Rendered zone content. The response body shape varies by the zone's creative configuration: HTML, JavaScript, JSON, or plain text. `X-Extole-*` response headers carry the matched campaign, frontend controller, creative, creative version, and input event identifiers, plus optional debug logs (`X-Extole-Log`).

200.description: Rendered zone content. post.description: Uploads a file and creates a file asset record. The request is a multipart/form-data body containing the file content and optional metadata (name, tags, format). post.description: Creates a new SFTP destination connection profile used for automated report file delivery. Returns the created destination with its server-assigned id.

type.description: Authentication shape backing the token. `USER` represents a human dashboard user, `MANAGED` an OAuth-style managed identity, and `RESOURCE` a scoped per-resource token.

access_token.description: Token string. Send as `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` on subsequent requests, or as the `access_token` query parameter / `extole_token` cookie.

client_id.description: Stable Extole identifier for the client (tenant) this token authenticates against.

identity_id.description: Stable Extole identifier for the identity (user, managed identity, or resource) that this token represents.

expires_in.description: Seconds until this token expires. Once expired, requests using it return `401 invalid_access_token`; rotate via `PUT /v4/tokens/exchange/{token}` before expiry to keep long-lived integrations alive.

scopes.description: Authorization scopes granted to this token. Determines which API operations the token may invoke.

items.description: Authorization scopes granted to this token. Determines which API operations the token may invoke.

person_id.description: Deprecated alias for `identity_id`. New integrations should use `identity_id`.

AccessTokenResponse.description: Access-token metadata returned by `POST /v4/tokens`, `POST /v4/tokens/openid-connect/authorization-code-flow`, `GET /v4/tokens`, `GET /v4/tokens/{token}`, and `PUT /v4/tokens/exchange/{token}`. Pass `access_token` in the `Authorization` header (`Bearer ...`) on subsequent requests.

client_id.description: Stable Extole identifier for the client (tenant) the new token should authenticate against. Required when authenticating with email/password credentials; optional when the calling identity already implies the client.

email.description: Email address of the dashboard user to authenticate. Pair with `password`. Returns `403 invalid_credentials` if the pair is wrong.

password.description: Password for the dashboard user identified by `email`. Returns `403 invalid_credentials` if wrong, `403 expired_credentials` if expired, `403 account_locked` if the account is locked, and `403 account_disabled` if disabled.

scopes.description: Subset of the calling identity's scopes to grant on the new token. Must be a strict subset; requesting a privilege the caller does not hold returns `403 scopes_denied` with the offending scopes in `denied_scopes`. Omit to mirror the caller's scopes.

items.description: Subset of the calling identity's scopes to grant on the new token. Must be a strict subset; requesting a privilege the caller does not hold returns `403 scopes_denied` with the offending scopes in `denied_scopes`. Omit to mirror the caller's scopes.

duration_seconds.description: Override the default token lifetime, in seconds. Must keep the token's expiry within the next ten millennia; out-of-range values return `400 invalid_duration` with the default lifetime in `default_duration`.

AccessTokenCreationRequest.description: Optional body for `POST /v4/tokens`. Omit the body entirely to mirror the calling identity's scopes; supply a body to bind the new token to a specific `client_id`, narrow its `scopes`, override the default lifetime via `duration_seconds`, or authenticate with email/password credentials in lieu of a calling token.
